name: Viranus Donton
bio:
Physical Appearance: Imperial male. Wears a complete set of steel armor except for the helmet - full heavy protection that marks him as a trained fighter. Wields a silver longsword and carries his house key.
Race: Imperial
Personality Keywords: isolated, frustrated, stifled, insecure, confined
Role: Viranus Donton is a member of the Chorrol Fighters Guild and son of Vilena Donton, the Master of the guild. Despite his training and equipment, he is not permitted to participate in any guild contracts due to his mother's restrictions following the death of his older brother Vitellus.
Home: Viranus lives in his mother Vilena's house on Great Oak Place in Chorrol. He sleeps on the second floor and spends his days confined to the house, rarely leaving except for Sunday chapel visits.
Family Loss: Viranus had an older brother, Vitellus, who was recently killed in the line of duty with the Fighters Guild. After this loss, Vilena refuses to allow Viranus to take any contracts or engage in guild work. He is described as not being "as confident as his brother was."
Daily Routine: Viranus sleeps from 2am to 8am on the second floor, then wakes and wanders around the ground floor. At noon he sits down for a two-hour lunch alone. He spends four more hours doing nothing, then sits for a two-hour dinner at 6pm. His evening is spent wandering. When Vilena returns home at midnight, she goes straight to bed, leaving him to himself until his bedtime at 2am. His only time outside the house is at 10am on Sundas when he spends two hours at the Chapel of Stendarr.
Combat Abilities: Viranus possesses a leveled set of knight spells, indicating proper training in both martial and magical combat. His steel armor and silver longsword show he's equipped for fighting, though he's not allowed to use this training.
His Journal: During his time in Forsaken Mine, Viranus carries a journal that describes "his many frustrations towards his overprotective mother" and hints at a relationship with his friend Eduard. The journal also contains information that becomes crucial to exposing the Blackwood Company's crimes.
Personality - The Confined Fighter: Viranus lives what the source describes as "a completely isolated existence" in his mother's house. His schedule consists of wandering the ground floor, eating meals alone, and doing "absolutely nothing" for hours at a time. Vilena "never allows him to participate in any contracts for the Fighters Guild." His journal describes frustrations toward his overprotective mother. During The Master's Son quest, he requires a boost in morale, suggesting his confidence needs external support.
The Overprotection: Vilena "understandably wants to keep her only remaining son alive" after losing Vitellus. She uses this as a reason to prevent Viranus from doing any guild jobs. When Vilena comes home at midnight, she goes straight to bed without spending time with him.
author: Bethesda
